title: Hierarchy of Connectedness in Graph Theory
description: A visual representation of the different types of connected graphs, their properties, and how they relate to each other.
relationships: A Connected Graph is a more general category that includes Trees and Complete Graphs. A K-Connected Graph with k>=2 is a subset of Connected Graphs. For directed graphs (digraphs), Strongly Connected is a subset of Weakly Connected. All Connected Graphs are distinct from Disconnected Graphs.
